sections.paragraph:
μεγαλωσύνη, ης, ἡ (s. prec. and next entry; Herodian, Gramm. I 335, 18; LXX; En 98:2; 101:3; the Gizeh text [5:4] has μεγαλοσύνη; EpArist 192; TestLevi 3:9; 18:8; Ar. 15, 2; Suda; Etym. Mag. p. 275, 44; Byz. Chron. in Psaltes p. 267) a state of greatness or preeminence, majesty, used only of God; in a doxology w. δόξα (and other sim. ideas; En 14:16) Jd 25; 1 Cl 20:12; 61:3; 64; 65:2; MPol 20:2; 21:1 (here, as Ar. 15, 2, referred to Christ). τὸ σκῆπτρον τῆς μ. τοῦ θεοῦ the scepter of the majesty of God 1 Cl 16:2; ἐν λόγῳ τῆς μ. by God’s majestic word 27:4. ἀπαύγασμα τῆς μ. a reflection of God’s majesty 36:2 (cp. Hb 1:3). τὸ τῆς μ. ὄνομα αὐτοῦ God’s glorious name 58:1.—As a periphrasis for God himself ἐν δεξιᾷ τῆς μ. at the right hand of God’s Majesty Hb 1:3. ὁ θρόνος τῆς μ. 8:1.—DELG s.v. μέγας. M-M. TW. Spicq.

prec. prec. = preceding

Herodian Herodian = Herodianus Historicus, III a.d.—List 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

En En = I Enoch—List 2

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

TestLevi TestLevi = Testament of Levi, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

Suda Suda = a lexical work (freq. cited as Suidas in handbooks); X a.d.—List 5

Mag.

Mag. = De Magistratibus Populi Romani

mag. = magic(al)

Byz. Byz. = Byzantine, esp. in ref. to papyri

sim. sim. = similarly

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 623
